questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word in bold.Elisabeth stood at the piano, which did nothing to allay his anxiety.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.If you want to be a good detective, it helps to have an <u>inquisitive</u> nature.
Identify the most appropriate antonym of the given word.Scary
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Miserable
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.She was <u>elated</u> about her promotion, but her colleague felt quite disheartened.
Identify the most appropriate antonym of the given word.Timid

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence to fill in the blank.Most of the land near the town is <u>barren</u>, but the efforts of the farmers have turned it ________.

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence to fill in the blank.Most of the flowers <u>bloom</u> in the spring season but they start to ________ during summer.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.Their sporting achievements at the highest level are being <u>utterly</u> ignored.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Risible
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Mechanical
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word in the brackets to fill in the blank.The ______ to the venue was decorated with beautiful lights. [exit]
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the following word.Forbid
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.He was very <u>cruel</u> in dealing with the students.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Meander
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence. In Shakespeare's The Merchant of Venice, Shylock's connivance aimed to <u>jeopardise</u> Antonio's life.
Select the word OPPOSITE in meaning to the given word. Deficient
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.<u>Success</u> is not a destination, it’s a journey.

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Receive
Identity the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Secure

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word in brackets to fill in the blank.She has _______ (witty) remarks about everything.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Monotonous
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.One must be very <u>strong</u> to lift this box of glass jars.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Catastrophe
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Barbarity
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word in bold.His mother never held a grudge against his father.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word to fill in the blank.The modern work is deliberately ________ (ambiguous).
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.An elephant is a <u>large</u> mammal.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Epitome
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.I was <u>happy</u> to know that Sheila passed her final exams.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word in brackets to fill in the blank.Sheila ________ (failed) all her classes.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word in the brackets to fill in the blank.She ________ the target by an inch and lost the gold medal. [hit]
Choose the most appropriate ANTONYM for the underlined word in the given sentence.The war was followed by a long period of <u>prosperity</u>.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Affirmative
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word in the brackets to fill in the blank.Akash went to ______ his school uniform. [sell]
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.If someone <u>babbles</u>, it means that they talk in a confused or excited way.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Entire
Select the word OPPOSITE in meaning to the underlined word in the given sentence.Feeling <u>discouraged</u>,̲ she finally finds the motivation for pursing her goal.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.We should plan our actions <u>judiciously</u> before going to war.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word in the brackets to fill in the blank.The chef requires ______ vegetables for the salad. [stale]
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Attack
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Synthetic
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the sentence given below. I <u>refuse</u> to let anything come in the way of my dreams.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The patient was suffering from a <u>chronic</u> disease.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence.The movie's plot was <u>predictable</u>, with clichéd twists and turns.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word in bold.Many people fought to abolish slavery during the Civil War.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Candid
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Reluctant
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The appearance of most of the animals in jungle is <u>deceptive</u> in nature.
